general: you're watching a website pretend to be a TV. we're both okay with this.
nobody chose this. that's the point.
this channel has one (1) viewer. hi.
no algorithm knows you're here. enjoy the privacy.
snow-globe has no CEO, no roadmap, and no notes.
the snow is real. the globe is a metaphor. the TV is a website.
we interrupt this program for nothing in particular.

left on. like a porch light for no one in particular.

there is no next episode. there is only now. and then more now.
do not adjust your set. the set is fine. you, however.
broadcasting from a laptop dangerously close to a glass of water.
no ads. no feed. no notes. no idea.
somewhere a small server is warm because of you. thank it.
if you can read this, the JavaScript worked. a minor miracle.
this is not content. this is television.
Anna made this instead of sleeping.
every show here was picked on purpose, by a person, with opinions.
you found the weird part of the internet. wipe your feet.
the signal is the show.
please enjoy responsibly. or don't. we genuinely cannot see you.
we'll be right back. we never left.
a syntax tree fell in a forest. it parsed beautifully.
consciousness is probably fine. back to the program.
this program contains traces of meaning. handle accordingly.
remember: it's only weird if you stop watching.
no thoughts. just broadcast.
morning: good morning. you're early. that's practically a personality.
the day hasn't started yet. you can still escape.
it's [time]. the sun did that thing again.
morning programming for people avoiding their inbox.
somewhere a French press is being plunged with real feeling.

rise. shine is optional and frankly ambitious.

you could be doing something else. bold of you not to.

halfway through the day, halfway through the loop.
the 2pm slump is a documented linguistic universal. probably.
it's [time]. this is fine. this is, in fact, television.
evening: the sun is setting somewhere. show-off.
evening programming: now with 40% more melancholy.
it's [time]. you earned this. or you didn't. we don't audit.
dinner and television: a load-bearing tradition.
the night is young. the playlist is older than it looks.
it's [time]. the good shows come out after dark, like raccoons.
latenight: it's [time]. why are you still up. (genuinely asking.)
late night: when the internet finally takes its shoes off.
everyone else went to bed. cowards.
3am is a place, not a time. welcome.
you and the insomniacs: a fellowship of the ringing ears.
the late hours belong to the curious and the caffeinated.
nothing good happens after 2am. except this. allegedly.
it's [time]. the thoughts are getting loud. let them watch TV.
deadhours: the dead hours. nothing is expected of you. truly nothing.
static. signal. static. signal. it's giving Morse.
the world is asleep. the broadcast does not care.
ambient hours. let it wash over you like lukewarm radio.

deep night. even the algorithms clocked out.
this is the channel the moon watches.
